children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: The Secretary of Agriculture is authorized to provide assistance under this section to eligible landowners who suffer destruction of 35 percent or more of a commercial tree stand due to damaging weather, related condition, or wildfire.
reimbursement of up to 65 percent of the cost of reestablishing such tree stand damaged by the damaging weather, related condition, or wildfire in excess of 35 percent mortality; or
at the discretion of the Secretary, provision of sufficient tree seedlings to reestablish such tree stand.

children.heading: Limitation on assistance
children.label: 1 Limitation on assistance
children.text: No person may receive an amount in excess of $25,000 in any fiscal year, or an equivalent value in tree seedlings, under this section.

children.heading: Ineligibility
children.label: 2 Ineligibility
children.text: A person who has qualifying gross revenues in excess of $2,000,000 annually, as determined by the Secretary, shall not be eligible to receive any disaster payment or other benefits under this section.

children.label: A
children.text: defining the term “person” for the purposes of this section that shall conform, to the extent practicable, to the regulations defining the term “person” issued under section 1308 of title 7 (before the amendment made by section 1603(a) of the Food, Conservation, and Energy Act of 2008);1See References in Text note below.1

children.label: B
children.text: prescribing such rules as the Secretary determines necessary to ensure a fair and reasonable application of the limitations established under this subsection; and

children.label: C
children.text: ensuring that no person receives duplicative payments or assistance under this section, the Cooperative Forestry Assistance Act of 1978 ( et seq.), and the environmental quality incentives program established under subchapter A of chapter 4 of subtitle D of title XII of the Food Security Act of 1985 [ et seq.], or other Federal program.16 U.S.C. 210116 U.S.C. 3839aa
the term “damaging weather” includes drought, hail, excessive moisture, freeze, tornado, hurricane, excessive wind, or any combination thereof;

children.label: A
children.text: produces annual crops from trees for commercial purposes and owns 500 acres or less of such trees;

children.label: B
children.text: owns 1,000 acres or less of private forest land; or

children.label: C
children.text: owns more than 1,000 acres but less than 5,000 acres of private forest land if the Secretary, in the Secretary’s discretion, determines the person eligible;

children.label: A
children.text: if a majority of the person’s annual income is received from farming, ranching, and forestry operations, the gross revenue from the person’s farming, ranching, and forestry operations; and

children.label: B
children.text: if less than a majority of the person’s annual income is received from farming, ranching, and forestry operations, the person’s gross revenue from all sources;
the term “related condition” includes insect infestations, disease, or other deterioration of a tree stand that is accelerated or exacerbated by damaging weather;

the term “reestablish” includes site preparation, reforestation of a damaged stand, and timber stand improvement practices, including thinning, prescribed burning, and other practices approved by the Secretary for reforestation;
the term “Secretary” means the Secretary of Agriculture; and
the term “wildfire” means any forest or range fire.

children.heading: Retroactive assistance
children.label: e Retroactive assistance
children.text: The Secretary shall use funds provided under this section to reimburse landowners for approved reforestation practices that were implemented before . The Secretary shall not make reimbursements for reforestation practices that were implemented prior to .November 28, 1990September 1, 1989
